answers.field_label: Additional Total Comments Line
answers.value: Totals include primary residence, two vehicles, and available credit lines; liabilities reflect outstanding mortgage, tribal loans, auto loan, and personal/credit card debts as of the date signed.
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.field_label: Customer Comments Line 1
answers.value: I am submitting this personal financial statement in connection with an application for a Spokane Tribal Credit long-term consolidation/household improvement loan. I want to clearly explain my current financial situation and how I manage my obligations month to month.
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.field_label: Customer Comments Line 2
answers.value: I have lived at my current home on the Spokane Reservation for almost 9 years. I have never missed a mortgage payment; when I was temporarily laid off during the COVID-19 pandemic, I used my emergency savings and a short-term tribal relief grant to stay current on all housing-related bills. Since returning to full-time work as an administrative coordinator for a regional health clinic, my income has been stable and I have been steadily paying down both my mortgage and my existing Spokane Tribal Credit long-term loan.
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.field_label: Customer Comments Line 3
answers.value: My main reason for seeking additional credit at this time is to consolidate several higher-interest debts (credit cards and a small personal loan) into a single, more manageable monthly payment and to complete a few necessary home improvements, including replacing aging windows and updating insulation to reduce heating costs in the winter. These improvements should help lower my monthly utility bills and protect the value of the home, which is my primary asset and where I am raising my two children.
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.field_label: Customer Comments Line 4
answers.value: I budget carefully every month, using a detailed spreadsheet to track all income and expenses. After covering my mortgage, utilities, insurance, groceries, and transportation, I consistently set aside a small amount into savings and an emergency fund. When I have extra funds (for example, from occasional overtime at the clinic or tax refunds), I apply them directly toward principal on higher-interest debts. I understand the importance of honoring my obligations to the Tribe and to all lenders, and I have not declared bankruptcy or defaulted on any loans.
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.field_label: Customer Comments Line 5
answers.value: If there is any information on this form that appears unclear or if you need supporting documents (pay stubs, bank statements, or account summaries), I am more than willing to provide them promptly. I appreciate the opportunity to work with Spokane Tribal Credit and I am committed to using any approved funds responsibly to strengthen my long-term financial stability and improve my home for my family.
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form
